era: 1990-2003. Garage Rock. E=7 -- loud, fast, irreverent, stripped down. Fremont. Amphetamine Reptile.

key_releases.title: The Inhuman Ordeal of Special Agent Gas Huffer
key_releases.label: Amphetamine Reptile
key_releases.significance: Title is the message. One of the best records.

key_releases.title: We've Come for Your Children
key_releases.label: Amphetamine Reptile
key_releases.significance: Late period. Still loud, still irreverent.

theory_nodes.concept: Tom Price / U-Men lineage. U-Men (1981-1989): pre-grunge Seattle post-punk, Birthday Party influence, noise-rock aesthetic. Price brings U-Men vocabulary into Gas Huffer's garage rock framework. The result is louder and more confrontational than typical garage rock. Underground lineage continuity: early-1980s Seattle underground through grunge era and beyond.
theory_nodes.description: U-Men to Gas Huffer: underground aesthetic continuity across two decades.

theory_nodes.concept: Amphetamine Reptile Records as primary AmRep representation in engine. Founded Minneapolis 1986 by Tom Hazelmyer. Noise-rock definitive label: Helmet, Halo of Flies, Melvins, Cows, Thrown Ups. High distortion, medium-fi production, personal A&R. Gas Huffer is the garage rock voice in a noise-rock catalog.
theory_nodes.description: AmRep: primary representation of noise-rock label culture in the engine.

theory_nodes.concept: E=7 two mechanisms: Screaming Trees (261) via psychedelic density; Gas Huffer (262) via speed and volume. Same energy level, entirely different approach. The engine's E-scale is not a single axis of loudness but a measure of presence and impact reachable by multiple routes.
theory_nodes.description: E=7 streak: density vs. speed as two paths to the same energy reading.

theory_nodes.concept: Humor as deliberate mode. Gas Huffer is frequently funny in a scene dominated by emotional weight and philosophical seriousness. Fremont absurdist context. This irreverence is a counterpoint to grunge's received mythology. Being loud and funny simultaneously is rarer than being loud and heavy.
theory_nodes.description: Irreverence as deliberate artistic mode in the Seattle scene.

cross_references.relationship: E=7 holds. Psychedelic Rock (Ellensburg) to Garage Rock (Fremont). Density mechanism to speed mechanism.
E=7 continues. Garage to Post-Hardcore. Fremont to Boise-to-Seattle.

sps_pair.pairing_note: E=7 + E=7. Both are large, physical, unstoppable forces. The lahar does not stop; the Gas Huffer song does not stop. Full-volume forward momentum as the shared mode.

milestones: PRIMARY AMPHETAMINE REPTILE REPRESENTATION IN ENGINE
